sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.40 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: (I.\,R.\,Shafarevich). Let$SL_2( )\,$and$SL_2( )^{-}$denote the completions of$SL_2( )$determined by all subgroups of finite index and all congruence subgroups, respectively, and let$: SL_2( )\, SL_2( )^{-}$be the natural homomorphism. Is${ Ker}\,$a free profinite group? { V.\,P.\,Platonov} Yes, it is (O.\,V.\,Mel'nikov, Soviet Math. Dokl., { 17} (1976), 867--870).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
